root_cause.summary: The `pnpm audit` step in pnpm/pnpm's Audit workflow reported 1 moderate-severity vulnerability — `ip-address has XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods` (advisory GHSA-v2v4-37r5-5v8g) — in the transitive dependency `ip-address` at vulnerable versions `<=10.1.0`, with patched range `>=10.1.1`. The dependency reaches the project through the path `network__fetch > @pnpm/network.agent > @pnpm/network.proxy-agent > socks-proxy-agent > socks > ip-address`. By default pnpm audit exits 1 on any vulnerability finding, which propagates to the GHA job's `##[error]Process completed with exit code 1.`. This is an audit/security gate failure, not an install failure — deps install fine; the fix is to bump the transitive `ip-address` to >=10.1.1.

required_signals.value: ip-address has XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods

evidence_spans.reason: GHA `##[group]Run pnpm audit` + the `pnpm audit` command line — establishes which step triggered the failure.

The full ascii-table vulnerability report from pnpm audit: severity (moderate), vulnerability title (`ip-address has XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods`), Package, Vulnerable versions (<=10.1.0), Patched versions (>=10.1.1), the transitive dependency Path, and the GHSA advisory URL. Sufficient on its own to identify root cause, the affected package, the fix (bump to >=10.1.1), and the dependency path.

Summary lines (`1 vulnerabilities found`, `Severity: 1 moderate`) plus the GHA `##[error]Process completed with exit code 1.` annotation that confirms the audit failure propagated to the job exit code.
